description: Audit components in text, then redraw enforcing the audit counts
prompt_template:
You are producing a clearer version of a 3D printer assembly manual page-crop.

Do this in TWO phases internally before producing the image:

PHASE 1 (audit, do not output as text):
  - Count every distinct component visible: each screw (by length / type),
    each washer, each plate, each printed-circuit isolator, each arrow.
  - Note the step number, every label string, every instruction line.
  - Note the X-LOCK wordmark position.

PHASE 2 (redraw enforcing the audit):
  - Produce a clearer version of the SAME diagram. Style may change
    (sharper lines, better contrast, even white background if helpful).
  - The redrawn image MUST contain the exact same count of every screw
    and component you noted in Phase 1. Adding or dropping even ONE screw
    is a failure.
  - Every label string and instruction line must appear with the same
    wording, in the same approximate position.
  - The step number circle stays in the same corner.
  - Aspect ratio matches the input.

Output ONLY the redrawn image, no commentary, no audit text.
